items.title: Dictionaries & Hash Maps
items.description: Sets and dicts both use hash tables — a set is essentially a dict that stores only keys.

items.title: Hash Tables
items.description: The abstract data type page covers collisions, load factor and resizing — the machinery that gives sets their O(1) operations.

items.title: Arrays & Lists
items.description: Lists check membership in O(n) via scanning; sets do it in O(1). Convert a list to a set when you'll test membership many times.

items.title: Tuples
items.description: Tuples of hashables are valid set elements — useful for storing visited (state, key) pairs.
